pr.title: [WIP] Decomp Progress: Full Session Delta (Big PR — Pruning & Splitting Down)
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Closed, unmerged WIP draft PR opened as a large full-session decomp progress snapshot intended to be pruned and split into smaller per-subsystem PRs. The loaded slice has no diff and no changed-file metadata, but the PR body claims 121 files touched across many subsystems, 119 new exact matches, 229 improved functions, and no local regressions; the bot report separately reports GALE01 gains of 132 new matches, 254 improvements, +82232 matched-code bytes, and +2440 matched-data bytes.

what_changed: PR body describes the branch as a deliberately large WIP decomp delta intended to be pruned and split into smaller reviewable per-subsystem PRs.
PR body claims local results needing validation: 119 new exact function matches and 229 improved functions.

PR body claims 121 files touched across gm, gr, mn, ft, cm, if, lb, mp, ty, it, sysdolphin, and extern/dolphin (THP), but the loaded metadata reports changed_files: 0 and the loaded raw diff is empty.
PR body claims local standing after the delta of 74.78% matched code, 97.88% fuzzy, and 18,942 / 19,829 functions matched.
PR body claims verification against a clean baseline build of current master with 0 broken matches and 0 regressions.
Bot report for GALE01 a384731..a74ae86 reports matched code 74.78% (+2.12%, +82232 bytes) and matched data 42.01% (+0.20%, +2440 bytes).
Bot report lists 132 new matches and 254 improvements in unmatched items, which differs from the PR author's body counts of 119 new exact matches and 229 improved functions.

smart_moves.text: The author explicitly marked the large delta as WIP and asked reviewers not to spend review effort on it before pruning, reducing review burden for an unreviewable aggregate branch.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: note says the PR will be pruned and split and asks not to spend review effort in this form

smart_moves.text: The author invited others to cherry-pick useful pieces and offered to patch merge conflicts, treating the branch as a shareable staging area rather than a final integration unit.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: Please Steal Pieces that Help Your Work section

smart_moves.text: The PR body separated local claims from validation status by labeling the results 'LOCAL Results to Be Validated'.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: What's in here section

smart_moves.text: The included bot report gave a concise before/after ledger with units, items, byte deltas, and percentages, which is useful for selecting candidate follow-up PRs.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment decomp-dev[bot]: GALE01 report tables

decomp_lessons.text: Large mixed-subsystem decomp progress branches are better treated as discovery or staging snapshots and then split into smaller per-subsystem PRs before review.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: deliberately big PR will be pruned down and split into smaller, reviewable per-subsystem PRs

decomp_lessons.text: For aggregate progress snapshots, keep local match claims provisional until diff evidence, file metadata, and focused validation are available.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: LOCAL Results to Be Validated
raw_diff.diff: empty loaded diff
metadata_json: changed_files 0

naming_conventions.text: No new naming convention is supported by the loaded PR slice. The bot report preserves existing project symbol styles such as grGreens_80213C10, ftCo_8009DD94, HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize, and fn_*/address-style unknown names, but there is no diff or review evidence explaining naming choices.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment decomp-dev[bot]: reported item names

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Use progress reports that list unit, item, byte delta, and before/after percentages to identify high-value functions or data items for follow-up extraction.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment decomp-dev[bot]: GALE01 report tables

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Treat clean-baseline and no-regression claims as useful but provisional when the slice lacks the diff and exact verification artifacts.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: verified against clean baseline build with 0 broken matches, 0 regressions
raw_diff.diff: empty loaded diff

review_feedback.text: No review comments were present in the loaded slice.
evidence_refs: metadata_json: review_comments 0 and reviews 0

review_feedback.text: Author-side review guidance: do not spend review effort on this PR in its current form because it is deliberately large and intended for pruning and splitting.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: author note

follow_up_queries: Find smaller PRs split from PR #2672 or branch progress/full-delta-2026-06-12.
Search for cherry-picked follow-up PRs containing grGreens_80213C10, grInishie1_801FAD84, fn_801785B0, ftCo_8009DD94, or grRCruise_80200C04.
Search later PRs by fjooord around 2026-06-14 for gm, gr, mn, ft, lb, mp, it, sysdolphin, and extern/dolphin subsystem splits.
Compare PR #2672 bot report counts with later focused PR reports to identify which functions actually landed.
Look for follow-up validation evidence for the claimed 0 broken matches and 0 regressions.

notes: The loaded slice preserves PR identity and textual progress reports but contains no actual diff and no changed-file metadata.
The PR was closed unmerged and marked draft in raw PR JSON; no acceptance or merge should be inferred.
Author claims and bot report counts differ: 119 vs 132 new matches, and 229 vs 254 improvements. This record keeps both as reported rather than reconciling them.
The PR body says 121 files were touched, but the metadata says changed_files 0 and the raw diff is empty; file-level changed paths cannot be confirmed from this slice.
No review comments are available, so reviewer intent and accepted lessons cannot be inferred.
Verification claims are author-reported; no build log, objdiff, or checkdiff artifact is loaded.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split aggregate decomp progress into reviewable subsystem PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: A full-session branch touching many subsystems can be useful as a staging or sharing point, but the PR itself should be pruned and split into smaller per-subsystem review units before normal review.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: author note about pruning and splitting

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep aggregate progress counts provisional without diff artifacts
accepted_candidate_records.text: When a PR slice only has body/report claims and no diff or changed-file metadata, store match counts as reported claims and avoid promoting individual functions as landed or accepted.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: LOCAL Results to Be Validated
raw_diff.diff: empty
metadata_json: changed_files 0

rejection_notes: Do not add the listed units as confirmed changed files; the slice lacks changed-file metadata and diff content.
Do not promote any of the reported function matches as accepted or landed from this PR; it was closed unmerged and no diff or focused validation artifact is loaded.
Do not infer naming standards from the listed symbol names; they appear only in a bot report table without code or review rationale.
Do not treat the author-reported 0 regressions as independently verified by this intake; no build or objdiff log is included.
